khBody.html:
Introduction

AEM's GraphQL API lets you query Content Fragments using typed, schema-driven queries. Unlike REST, you request exactly the fields you need.

Persisted Queries

Always use persisted queries in production — they are executed via HTTP GET, making them fully cacheable at the CDN layer.

items.khSummary: A practical guide to querying Content Fragments using AEM's GraphQL API and persisted queries.
items.khSeoDescription: Learn how to use AEM GraphQL API to query Content Fragments with persisted queries for headless delivery.

khBio.plaintext: Senior AEM Architect with 10+ years of experience in Adobe Experience Cloud implementations.

khRelatedFAQs.khQuestion: What is a persisted query and why should I use it?

khAnswer.html: A persisted query is a GraphQL query saved on the AEM server and executed via HTTP GET. This makes it cacheable at CDN level — unlike POST-based queries which bypass the cache entirely.
